tmc/udn.h File Reference

Support for the UDN (user dynamic network). More...


Defines

#define TMC_UDN0_DEMUX_TAG   UDN0_DEMUX_TAG
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 0.
#define TMC_UDN1_DEMUX_TAG   UDN1_DEMUX_TAG
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 1.
#define TMC_UDN2_DEMUX_TAG   UDN2_DEMUX_TAG
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 2.
#define TMC_UDN3_DEMUX_TAG   UDN3_DEMUX_TAG
 The default tag for UDN demux queue 3.

Functions

int tmc_udn_init (cpu_set_t *cpus)
 Initialize the hardwall using a rectangle containing cpus.
int tmc_udn_close (void)
 Stop using any created (or inherited) hardwall.
int tmc_udn_activate (void)
 Activate the hardwall to provide access to the network.
int tmc_udn_persist_after_exec (int flag)
 Set whether or not the hardwall should be available after exec().
DynamicHeader tmc_udn_header_from_cpu (int cpu)
 Convert a CPU number to a dynamic network header.
void tmc_udn_send_buffer (DynamicHeader dest, unsigned long tag, const void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Send a packet of words on the UDN.
void tmc_udn0_receive_buffer (void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 0.
void tmc_udn1_receive_buffer (void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 1.
void tmc_udn2_receive_buffer (void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 2.
void tmc_udn3_receive_buffer (void *buf, unsigned long words)
 Receive a packet of words from UDN demux 3.
void tmc_udn_send (uint_reg_t word)
 Inject a word into the UDN.
void tmc_udn_send_1 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0)
 Send a packet of one word.
void tmc_udn_send_2 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1)
 Send a packet of two words.
void tmc_udn_send_3 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2)
 Send a packet of three words.
void tmc_udn_send_4 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2, uint_reg_t n3)
 Send a packet of four words.
void tmc_udn_send_5 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2, uint_reg_t n3, uint_reg_t n4)
 Send a packet of five words.
void tmc_udn_send_6 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2, uint_reg_t n3, uint_reg_t n4, uint_reg_t n5)
 Send a packet of six words.
void tmc_udn_send_7 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2, uint_reg_t n3, uint_reg_t n4, uint_reg_t n5, uint_reg_t n6)
 Send a packet of seven words.
void tmc_udn_send_8 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2, uint_reg_t n3, uint_reg_t n4, uint_reg_t n5, uint_reg_t n6, uint_reg_t n7)
 Send a packet of eight words.
void tmc_udn_send_9 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2, uint_reg_t n3, uint_reg_t n4, uint_reg_t n5, uint_reg_t n6, uint_reg_t n7, uint_reg_t n8)
 Send a packet of nine words.
void tmc_udn_send_10 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2, uint_reg_t n3, uint_reg_t n4, uint_reg_t n5, uint_reg_t n6, uint_reg_t n7, uint_reg_t n8, uint_reg_t n9)

void tmc_udn_send_20 (DynamicHeader dest, uint32_t tag, uint_reg_t n0, uint_reg_t n1, uint_reg_t n2, uint_reg_t n3, uint_reg_t n4, uint_reg_t n5, uint_reg_t n6, uint_reg_t n7, uint_reg_t n8, uint_reg_t n9, uint_reg_t n10, uint_reg_t n11, uint_reg_t n12, uint_reg_t n13, uint_reg_t n14, uint_reg_t n15, uint_reg_t n16, uint_reg_t n17, uint_reg_t n18, uint_reg_t n19)
 Send a packet of 20 words.
uint_reg_t tmc_udn0_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 0.
uint_reg_t tmc_udn1_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 1.
uint_reg_t tmc_udn2_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 2.
uint_reg_t tmc_udn3_receive (void)
 Receive a word from UDN demux queue 3.
unsigned int tmc_udn0_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 0.
unsigned int tmc_udn1_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 1.
unsigned int tmc_udn2_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 2.
unsigned int tmc_udn3_available_count (void)
 Get the number of words in UDN demux queue 3.
unsigned int tmc_udn_available_mask (void)
 Determine which UDN demuxes have data available.

Detailed Description

Support for the UDN (user dynamic network).

Third-party software: The Tilera IDE makes use of the BeanShell scripting library. Source code for the BeanShell library can be found at the BeanShell website (http://www.beanshell.org/developer.html).

The following is a trademark of Marvell Semiconductor, Inc.: Distributed Switching Architecture (DSA).
